title: thunbergia alata var. vixalata
intro: Thunbergia alata var. viridiflora, commonly known as the Black Eyed Susan Vine, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Traditionally, herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
